# tokens: # Principle: Multiple residual values on the stack are the result.
# AQL is a stack machine. A program is a sequence of tokens that push,
# pop, and transform stack entries. There is no distinguished 'return
# slot': whatever values remain on the stack at end-of-Run() are the
# result, in source order (first-pushed at the bottom, last-pushed
# on top).
# This file pins down the multivalue convention from several angles:
#  - Bare literals never converge to a single result.
#  - A handler that returns multiple values appends them in order.
#  - A paren group whose body produces multiple values transparently
#    surfaces them onto the outer stack (no 'tuple wrapping').
#  - Stack-only ops (dup, swap, drop) mutate only the values they
#    explicitly touch — pre-existing residency below them is
#    invariant.
# Why this matters
# Many languages with 'every expression has one value' semantics
# would silently coerce a multi-value residual into a tuple, a
# discard, or an error. AQL does not — leaving N values is a
# first-class outcome. Code generators and pretty-printers depend
# on this so a reader can mentally simulate the stack from the
# source without a wrapping layer to track.
# A subtler property pinned down here: paren-emitted values keep
# their relative order. `( 1 2 3 )` produces `1 2 3` on the outer
# stack (1 deepest, 3 on top), NOT a reversed `3 2 1` and NOT a
# packed list `[1 2 3]`. The paren is a syntactic boundary for
# evaluation, not a value-collection container.
# Spec runner notes
# The renderer joins the result stack with single spaces, so
# `1 2 3` here means three integers in that source order. The
# runner does NOT apply implicit auto-evaluation on bare literal
# stacks (only on Eval=true lists at end-of-run, which list
# residency does NOT trigger).
# === Test fixtures (defined in eng/go/spec_test.go::registerSpecWords) ===
#   addq   — [Number Number] forward-prec — args[1] + args[0] (decimal contagion)
#   tripq  — [Integer Integer Integer] forward-prec — 'a,b,c' (3-arg position mixing)
# === end fixtures ===

note: two values, two results — no implicit reduction

# tokens: 1 'two' 3
note: mixed types coexist on the stack

# tokens: # Stack-only words touch only their args — residue below is invariant.
1 2 3 dup
note: dup of the top extends the stack by one; deeper values unchanged

# tokens: 1 2 3 drop
note: drop removes the top; 1 and 2 stay where they were

# tokens: 1 2 3 swap
1 2 3 4 swap
note: with four-deep stack, swap still only touches top two

# tokens: # Paren groups producing N>1 values surface them onto the outer stack.

note: single-value paren is identity on count
three-value paren leaves all three; order preserved
nested: inner paren's values flatten into outer
paren in the middle interleaves correctly with surrounding literals

# tokens: ( 1 dup )
note: body produces a multivalue result via dup

# tokens: ( 1 2 swap )
note: body's swap output flows out as two separate stack entries

# tokens: # Combinations: handler return then more literals.
addq 2 3 99
note: addq returns one value; 99 sits on top of the result

# tokens: addq 2 3 dup
# Three-arg handler returning ONE value still leaves the result alone.
tripq 1 2 3 99
note: tripq returns one string; 99 stays after it
